text: The halo effect illustrates how System 1 constructs coherent narratives from fragmentary data. When we learn one positive fact about a person — that they are warm, for instance — System 1 immediately begins generating supplementary positive inferences: they are probably also generous, competent, honest. Kahneman traces this to a deeper principle he calls WYSIATI — What You See Is All There Is. System 1 does not wait for complete information; it builds the most coherent story possible from whatever is available, and then acts as if that story is the whole picture. Missing evidence is simply not missed.
question: What does Kahneman's acronym WYSIATI stand for?

options.text: What You Seek Is Always There In Analysis
What You See Is All There Is
When Your System Is Automatically Triggered In Action
What You Signal Is Assessed Through Intuition Alone
